mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, compressing required returns and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during rate cuts are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 8.0 percent, labor market deterioration signals severe corporate earnings headwinds and consumer demand collapse, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 55 percent of triggered instances, or the signal loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous rise in retail sales and decline in housing starts indicates consumer substitution away from durable goods and real estate, signaling demand fragility that drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns with an accuracy exceeding 55 percent, or the interaction term loses explanatory power in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Positive month-over-month growth in the index of industrial production confirms manufacturing sector stabilization, which directly supports equity valuations and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain negative or flat during periods of confirmed industrial production growth, or the signal shows zero predictive power in recovery regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Low nominal interest rates combined with declining inflation expectations compress real borrowing costs, stimulating corporate investment and equity risk appetite, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to generate positive 60-day returns in more than 60 percent of observations, or the interaction effect is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly contraction in real GDP exceeding 2.0 percent confirms severe aggregate demand destruction, forcing equity markets to price in prolonged earnings declines and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are positive in more than 50 percent of triggered quarters, or the threshold signal shows no edge over a simple mean-reversion baseline.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls reflect expanding economic activity and rising household income, which directly supports corporate revenue growth and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 55 percent of months with payroll growth, or the signal loses predictive accuracy in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer prices alongside stagnant industrial production creates a stagflationary supply shock that erodes corporate margins and equity valuations, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative 60-day returns with an accuracy above 55 percent, or the mechanism shows no statistical edge during high-inflation periods.
